This forty-year-old gentleman with a three-year history of Crohn's disease was admitted multiple times to the hospital during a three-month period for enteroenteric fistulizing disease.

The terminal ileum is positioned next to the colonic segment in an isoperistaltic orientation. A stay suture is applied to the right lateral aspect of the bowel and secured with a Carter-Thomson instrument to the right upper portion of the abdomen.

The surgeon closes the stapler insertion site using intracorporeal suturing, securing the area where the stapler was previously used.
